folio.label: 63v
folio.l: Thy blisseful eyen , saw al this turment
Tha~ne is there , no comparisone bytwene
Thy woo , and any woo , man may sustene
¢ Thow saw thy child I slayn , biforn~ thyne eyen
And ©et now lyuetú , my litel child ¹fay
Now lady brigút , to whom alle wooful cryen
Thow glorie of wommanhode , æè faire may
Thow hauene of refuyt , brigút´ sterre of day
Rewe on my childe , that of thy gentillesce
Rewest on euäy reuful/ in distresse
¢ O litel child , allas what is thy gilt
That neuäe wrougútest synne , as ©et pardee
Why wyl thyn fader , han the spilt
O mercy deere Constable , quod she
As lat my litel child , dwelle heere witú the
And if æè darst nou©t , sauen hi~ froorfroor blame
So kisse hi~ oones , in his fadres name
¢ There witú she loked bakward , to the londe
And seide farewel husbonde , routhelees
And vp she rist , and walketú doun the stronde
Toward the Ship~ , hire folwetú al the prees
And euäe she preyetú , hire child , to holde his pees
And takitú hire leue , and witú an hol entent
She blissed hire , and in to shipe she went
¢ Vetailled was the Ship~ , it is no drede
Habundauntly , for hire , ful longe space
And othere necessaries , that shulde nede
She had I now , heried be goddes grace
For wynd & weder , almygúty god purchace
And brynge hire home , I kan no betyr seye
But in the See , she dryuetú fortú hire weye
¢ Alla the kyngö , cometú hom sone after this
Vn to his Castel , of the whiche I tolde
And asketú where his wyf , and his child is
The Constable gan , a boute his hert colde
And pleynly al the maner , he hi~ tolde
As ©e han herd , I kan telle it no bettre
And shewetú the kyngö , his seel & his &lbar;re
¢ And seide , lord , as ©e comaunded me
Vp peyne of deetú , so haue I doon certeyne
This Messanger , turmented was , til he
Most byknowe , and tellen plat and pleyn
Fro nygút to nygút , in what place he had leyn
